behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "request sent to the external API" requires verifying module-to-client handoff, not just isolated string logic. MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at the boundary where taxonomy data is serialized into the outbound payload. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: no real network or model judgment is needed; a captured in-process stub request is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text does not require real HTTP conditions; Agentic rejected because it does not depend on model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier + request builder + API client adapter to a stub transport that records outbound payload; assert every call includes the full configured taxonomy label list in the API request fields.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is local validation/mapping logic: out-of-taxonomy label -> "unknown". No module interaction is required to reproduce the failure; direct function input can trigger it. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/validation function with a taxonomy and an invalid provider label; assert output is exactly "unknown" and never the raw invalid label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets "normalization step" determinism for identical input, which is pure local logic. No cross-module boundary is needed to expose nondeterministic normalization bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-style) with the same raw API payload fixture and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "repeated live API calls" and consistency tied to "provider response" require the real AI API path. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on actual model/provider outputs across runs; a stub would hide real drift/instability. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because deterministic fixtures cannot test real model decision consistency; Workflow rejected because this is repeated single-step calls, not a multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classification calls for the same document/taxonomy, store raw provider responses and final labels, and assert label stability when raw provider output is unchanged; flag divergence otherwise.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "before dispatch" indicates interaction between budget config/policy and request construction path. MOCK-MISS: boundary bugs can appear when constructor + policy module combine. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP: no real network/model output needed to catch enforcement failures. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected since behavior text does not require real OS/network; Agentic rejected since this tests pre-dispatch control logic, not model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Compose tokenizer/budget policy/request builder with an in-process fake dispatch sink; feed over-budget inputs and assert truncate-or-reject behavior is applied before any dispatch call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "Provider-reported token usage" requires a real AI provider response, not a local fake counter. LLM-DEP rule: execution path through real AI/ML API defaults to Agentic unless purely transport plumbing; this behavior checks model/provider accounting outcome. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because mocked usage can always be made compliant and hide real provider accounting mismatch; System considered but disqualified because the critical assertion is about AI-provider-reported model usage semantics, not generic HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls for representative documents and assert returned provider usage metrics (prompt/total tokens) do not exceed configured budget thresholds.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: QUOTE-EVIDENCE: "API signals...rate is too high" with "waits and retries" is retry orchestration across classifier + client + backoff policy modules. MOCK-MISS: needs multi-module handoff/state, but can be reproduced with stubbed 429 responses and controlled clock. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because real network is not required for minimum failure reproduction; Agentic rejected because correctness is retry control flow, not model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub provider that returns rate-limit response then success, with fake/controllable clock; assert the module delays per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing an immediate error.
